commentaries.author: Ambrosiaster
commentaries.content: The Corinthians were to eject this man not only by common consent among themselves but also in the power of Christ, whose deputy Paul was. Commentary on Paul’s Epistles.

commentaries.author: Cyprian of Carthage
commentaries.content: When, as soon as peace is given to us all by the Lord, we shall begin to return to the Church, then the wishes of each one shall be looked into in your presence, and with your judgment.

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: Paul does not allow the Corinthians to wait for him to come but passes sentence from afar. He did this so as not to give the Corinthians any leeway for making excuses. They were to act immediately and in the very precise manner described.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
